title: Pteris barklyae
intro: Pteris barklyae, also known as the Cape fern or the Barkly's fern, is a species of fern native to South Africa, classified under the genus Pteris. Traditionally, Pteris barklyae has been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin irritations, due to its anti-inflammatory, antipyretic, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Pteris barklyae is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and can also be found in the form of capsules or tinctures, commonly used to treat fever and respiratory infections.
taxonomy: Pteris barklyae is a species of the fern genus Pteris, belonging to the family Pteridaceae. This classification places it within the subfamily Pteridoideae. The genus Pteris is a group of ferns commonly known as the bracken ferns, and Pteris barklyae is one of the over 200 species within this group. The family Pteridaceae is a large and diverse group of ferns, with over 1,500 species found worldwide. Pteris barklyae, like other members of the Pteridaceae family, is an epiphytic or terrestrial fern.
